A critical statistic to note is that Carolina has not lost a single game in regulation this season when top defenseman Jaccob Slavin is in the lineup, boasting an 7.0--1.0-4.0 record in those contests. Slavin’s ability to neutralize top-tier opposing forwards will be vital against an Ottawa team that relies heavily on the offensive production of Tim StützleTim Stützle and Brady Tkachuk. Carolina goaltending has been top 6.0 in the NHL and Ottawa is only producing 20-23 shots. We are getting a great price to take a top team here. Ottawa, conversely, is struggling with significant defensive instability. The Senators currently rank in the bottom five of the league with a 4.4 Goals Against Average (GAA). This defensive vulnerability is further exacerbated by the absence of their primary netminder, Linus Ullmark, who is currently on Injured Reserve.
